title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: faster, cleaner, statistically richer successor SDALGCP. SDALGCP fits spatially discrete approximation log-Gaussian Cox process (SDA-LGCP) spatially aggregated disease counts, estimating parameters Monte Carlo Maximum Likelihood (MCML) predicting discrete continuous relative risk (Johnson, Diggle & Giorgi, 2019, Stat. Med. 38:4871–4887). document records (1) method , (2) current package slow statistically weak, (3) concrete plan SDALGCP2.

what: 1. The model and the approximation (so the speed work stays faithful)
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Disease counts Y_i regions A_i, = 1..N, modelled log-Gaussian Cox process aggregated area level: continuous areal integral ∫_{A_i} exp(S(x)) dx replaced discrete average candidate points placed inside region: Hence region-level random effect vector S* = (T_1,...,T_N) Gaussian N x N covariance Everything downstream (MCML, prediction) operates N x N object. candidate-point machinery exists build Sigma. split — expensive one-“build aggregated correlation” step, repeated cheap N x N linear algebra — key time spent. Estimation (Christensen 2004 MCML): 1. Pick anchor (phi0, sigma2_0), draw S* samples [S* | Y] Langevin–Hastings (MALA) sampler. 2. phi grid, maximise importance-sampling-corrected Monte Carlo log-likelihood (beta, sigma2) phi fixed. 3. Profile phi; report maximiser deviance-based CI. Spatio-temporal version: separable Sigma_space ⊗ Sigma_time, time correlation Matérn(kappa) range nu.
code: Y_i | S  ~  Poisson( m_i * exp( d_i' beta + T_i(S) ) ) S(.)      ~  zero-mean Gaussian process,  Cov{S(x),S(x')} = sigma2 * exp(-||x-x'||/phi) T_i(S) = sum_k w_ik S(x_ik)         (population-weighted, sum_k w_ik = 1)        = mean_k S(x_ik)             (unweighted) Sigma(phi, sigma2)_{ij} = sigma2 * sum_{k,l} w_ik w_jl exp(-||x_ik - x_jl|| / phi)

what: 2. Where SDALGCP is slow (measured against the algorithm above)
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Ranked impact.

previous_headings: 2. Where SDALGCP is slow (measured against the algorithm above)
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Pure-R double loop N(N+1)/2 region pairs; pair forms n_i x n_j distance matrix 3-D array exp(-outer(D, 1/phi)), reduces every phi. Cost ≈ O(N^2 * nbar^2 * n_phi) large constant factors R-level outer, array allocation, pdist. - PBC example (N = 545, ~tens points/region, 20 phi) wall-clock bottleneck allocates gigabytes transiently. - Fix: single RcppArmadillo kernel, OpenMP-parallel region pairs. Compute pairwise distance , accumulate weighted/mean exponential sum phi tight inner loop — 3-D array, per-phi R allocation. Expected 1–2 orders magnitude faster.

previous_headings: 2. Where SDALGCP is slow (measured against the algorithm above)
what: B2. Laplace.sampling — the MALA loop is interpreted R
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: n.sim (default 10 000) iterations, Sigma.sroot %*% W gradient products R. Runs estimation every prediction call. - Fix: port inner loop C++ (proposal density, gradient, accept step). Keep adaptation identical results match. ~10–30x sampler.

previous_headings: 2. Where SDALGCP is slow (measured against the algorithm above)
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: apply(S.sim, 1, ...) plus lapply/Reduce gradient Hessian rebuild quadratic forms sample--sample every optimiser step. - Fix: vectorise. S.sim (n.sim x N) Cholesky factor R, quadratic forms (S-mu)' R^{-1} (S-mu) one crossprod/triangular solve; gradients/Hessians become matrix ops. Factor R (Cholesky → log-det + solves), never call solve() inner loop.

previous_headings: 2. Where SDALGCP is slow (measured against the algorithm above)
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Aggregated_poisson_log_MCML_ST materialises kronecker(inv.t.corr, inv.s.corr) — (N*T) x (N*T) dense matrix — log-det/products every likelihood, gradient Hessian call. Kronecker structure unnecessary: Fix: never form Kronecker product; reshape sample T x N matrix use identities. Turns (N*T)^3 work N^3 + T^3 plus cheap matrix multiplies. single biggest ST win. Also drop geoR::varcov.spatial (orphaned dependency) temporal Matérn — compute Matérn directly lines / C++.
code: (A ⊗ B)^{-1} = A^{-1} ⊗ B^{-1} det(A ⊗ B)   = det(A)^{n_B} det(B)^{n_A} x'(A⊗B)^{-1}x = tr( B^{-1} X' A^{-1} X ),   X = mat(x)  (T x N or N x T)

previous_headings: 2. Where SDALGCP is slow (measured against the algorithm above)
what: B5. compute_cross_cov (continuous prediction) — R double loop
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: n.pred * N iterations calling pdist. Fix: C++ kernel mirroring B1.

previous_headings: 2. Where SDALGCP is slow (measured against the algorithm above)
what: B6. Pervasive solve() / determinant() instead of Cholesky
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Many sites invert full matrices separately compute determinants. Replace one Cholesky per matrix → triangular solves + 2*sum(log(diag(L))).

previous_headings: 2. Where SDALGCP is slow (measured against the algorithm above)
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Analytic gradient Hessian available Hessian -Sigma^{-1} - diag(h). handful damped Newton steps converge faster reliably BFGS. Fix: custom Newton supplied Hessian.

what: S1. General Matérn spatial covariance (not just exponential)
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: point-level kernel hard-wired exp(-d/phi) (Matérn kappa=0.5). Expose kappa (½, 1½, 2½ closed forms; general via Bessel) smoothness modelling choice, consistent temporal side already uses Matérn.

what: S2. A nugget / unstructured area effect (overdispersion)
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Add optional region-level ..d. term U_i ~ N(0, tau2) Sigma = sigma2 * R(phi) + tau2 * . Aggregated disease counts routinely show overdispersion beyond spatial signal; without nugget sigma2/phi absorb bias range. small change covariance assembly genuinely useful modelling option.

what: S3. Honest Monte Carlo error & importance-sampling diagnostics
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: MCML estimate conditional draws taken anchor phi0. Currently diagnostic trustworthy importance reweighting phi far phi0. - Report effective sample size importance weights chosen phi, ESS = (sum w)^2 / sum w^2, warn collapses. - Report Monte Carlo standard error maximised log-likelihood.

title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: first profile, re-anchor sampler current (phi, sigma2) optimum refit twice (stop estimates stabilise). fixes main failure mode single-pass MCML — wide phi grid far truth — modest extra cost, makes results far less sensitive par0.

what: S5. Proper profile CI for phi
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Replacing MC log-likelihood profile loess smooth taking deviance CI (current phiCI) smooths Monte Carlo noise genuine curvature together. Prefer monotone spline (MC-error-aware) profile points, finer adaptive phi grid near maximum, propagate MC error reported interval.

what: S6. Optional Laplace (no-MCMC) fast path for prediction
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Poisson-aggregated data Gaussian/Laplace approximation [S*|Y] typically excellent. Offer method = "laplace" skips MALA entirely prediction (mode + Gaussian covariance already computed maxim.integrand). Near-instant maps; keep MCMC accurate default.

title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Continuous/discrete prediction plugs MCML point estimates. Offer option propagate (beta, sigma2, phi) uncertainty (draw asymptotic / profile distribution) predictive SEs -confident.

title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Current default [sqrt(min area), extent/10] geometric arbitrary. Seed grid empirical variogram Poisson-GLM Pearson residuals grid brackets sensible range; still allow manual override.

title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Goal (user request): let covariates supplied rasters z(x) enter model correctly, first averaging predictor polygon plugging average region-level GLM. Naive areal aggregation (\bar z_i)' \beta biased whenever exp link nonlinear z varies within region — ignores Jensen’s inequality. Principle: aggregate intensity scale, linear-predictor scale. aggregated-LGCP mean region x_ik candidate points already used build Sigma w_ik (population) weights. Factor covariate part : approximate inner sum log-normal aggregate (linear functional S plus variance correction): giving model keeps fast N-dimensional structure: right contribution - b_i(beta) log-sum-exp within-region covariate surface — (\bar z_i)'beta, reduces z constant A_i. - spatial aggregation weights c_ik(beta) tilted covariate intensity, high-covariate sub-areas contribute region effect — statistically coherent behaviour. - collapses current SDALGCP exactly covariates region-constant (c_ik = w_ik, b_i = z_i'beta, v_i absorbed intercept). Computational consequence & plan. Sigma offset now depend beta, clean “precompute correlation , profile cheaply” loop becomes iterated scheme: () hold beta fixed → rebuild tilted weights → assemble Sigma(beta) b(beta) via existing C++ kernel (already takes arbitrary weights), (ii) run MCML step, (iii) update beta, iterate convergence. cheaper first-order option keeps c_ik = w_ik (covariate enters offset b_i(beta) v_i), leaving Sigma beta-independent fully precomputable — still strictly better areal averaging. offered; default first-order offset model, fully-tilted version option. Implementation tracked task #5; design fixed .
code: E[Y_i | S] = m_i ∫_{A_i} exp(z(x)'beta + S(x)) rho(x) dx            ≈ m_i * sum_k w_ik exp(z(x_ik)'beta + S(x_ik))          (discretised) = m_i * exp(b_i(beta)) * sum_k c_ik(beta) exp(S(x_ik)),   b_i(beta)  = log sum_k w_ik exp(z(x_ik)'beta)        # log-sum-exp covariate offset   c_ik(beta) = w_ik exp(z(x_ik)'beta) / exp(b_i(beta)) # covariate-tilted weights, sum_k = 1 sum_k c_ik exp(S(x_ik)) ≈ exp( T_i(S;beta) + 0.5 v_i(beta) ),   T_i(S;beta) = sum_k c_ik(beta) S(x_ik)               # covariate-tilted spatial avg   v_i(beta)   = Var of that aggregation (from the point-level covariance) E[Y_i | S*] = m_i * exp( b_i(beta) + T_i(S*;beta) + 0.5 v_i(beta) ), S* ~ N(0, Sigma(phi, sigma2; beta)),  Sigma built with the tilted weights c_ik.

title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Current behaviour. spatial scale phi discretised: evaluate aggregated correlation R(phi) grid, fit (beta, sigma2) , take profile maximum. grid robust (shows whole profile) wasteful — grid points far optimum — resolution caps precision phi-hat. Idea (user). Treat phi continuous parameter optimise directly inside MCML objective, alongside beta sigma2, instead scanning grid. MCML log-likelihood smooth phi, gradient-based optimiser needs R(phi) derivative dR/dphi arbitrary phi. Derivatives available closed form. aggregated exponential kernel, (general Matern: differentiate closed-form 3/2, 5/2 kernels similarly). MCML gradient w.r.t. log phi follows exactly existing spatio-temporal code, already temporal range nu: weighted importance weights reduced samples — vectorised machinery mcml_fit, plus one extra parameter. Plan. - New C++ kernel corr_and_grad_cpp(coords, weights, phi, kappa) returning R dR/dphi (reuses corr_aggregate inner loop). - mcml_fit(..., method = c("grid","direct")): "grid" stays default reference; "direct" optimises (beta, log sigma2, log phi) jointly nlminb/optim, recomputing R(phi)/R'(phi) per step (cheap now). - Standard errors phi come directly joint Hessian (loess/spline needed), profile plot can still produced demand. - Compare two: agreement phi-hat speed (direct need far fewer correlation builds 20-point grid). Keep options permanently — grid diagnostics/multimodal profiles, direct speed/precision. Tracked task #6; design fixed .
code: R_ij(phi)      = sum_{k,l} w_ik w_jl exp(-d_kl/phi) dR_ij/dphi     = sum_{k,l} w_ik w_jl exp(-d_kl/phi) * (d_kl / phi^2) d/dphi of the per-sample joint log-density   = -0.5 [ tr(R^{-1} R')  -  (S-mu)' R^{-1} R' R^{-1} (S-mu) / sigma2 ],   R' = dR/dphi

title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Drop legacy/orphaned deps: geoR (orphaned), sp, raster, spacetime, mapview, splancs, pdist, maxLik. Keep lean stack: sf, terra, stars, spatstat.geom/spatstat.random (sampling), Matrix, ggplot2, plus Rcpp/RcppArmadillo kernels. class(para_est) != "SDALGCP" — breaks multi-class objects; use inherits(). scale_fill_viridis_c(name = cat(...)) — cat() returns NULL; legend title silently dropped. aes_string() deprecated — use tidy-eval .data[[var]]. SDADiscretePred recomputes apply(.,2,sd) repeatedly exp(S.sim); cache. Reproducibility: thread explicit RNG seed samplers; document OpenMP reductions order-stable correlation build. Testing: testthat suite asserting SDALGCP2 reproduces SDALGCP estimates PBC data Monte-Carlo tolerance (correctness gate /optimising). Benchmarks: bench-based scripts comparing kernel old vs new.

title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
code: SDALGCP2/   DESCRIPTION            # lean deps + LinkingTo Rcpp, RcppArmadillo   NAMESPACE              # roxygen-generated   R/     covariance.R         # aggregated correlation assembly (R wrapper over C++), Matern, nugget     sampling.R           # Laplace mode (Newton) + MALA (C++-backed)     mcml.R               # vectorised MC likelihood, profile over phi, re-anchoring     fit.R                # SDALGCP2() user entry point (spatial)     fit_st.R             # spatio-temporal (Kronecker-free)     predict.R            # discrete + continuous prediction, Laplace fast path     points.R             # candidate-point generators (SSI / uniform / regular)     methods.R            # print/summary/confint/plot, exceedance     diagnostics.R        # ESS, MC error, profile CI for phi   src/     corr_aggregate.cpp   # B1: OpenMP aggregated correlation (FLAGSHIP, included)     mala_sampler.cpp     # B2     mc_loglik.cpp        # B3     kron_st.cpp          # B4 helpers     cross_cov.cpp        # B5   tests/testthat/   vignettes/   DESIGN.md              # this file

title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Keep user-facing verbs recognisable existing users consistent: SDALGCP2() (fit), predict() S3 method, controlmcmc(), summary(), confint(), phi_ci(), autoplot()/plot(). Provide short “migrating SDALGCP” section vignette.

what: 6. Expected impact (rough, to be confirmed by benchmarks)
title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Numbers targets; test suite gates correctness first, bench confirms speedups.

title: SDALGCP2 — Design & Improvement Proposal
text: Scaffold + flagship kernel (commit): package skeleton, DESIGN.md, corr_aggregate.cpp + R wrapper + correctness/benchmark check vs reference R implementation. Vectorised MC likelihood (B3) + Newton Laplace mode (B7). C++ MALA (B2), wire spatial fit end--end, testthat parity vs SDALGCP. Prediction (B5) + Laplace fast path (S6). Statistical extensions: nugget (S2), Matérn (S1), diagnostics (S3), re-anchor (S4). Spatio-temporal Kronecker-free path (B4). Vignette, benchmarks, docs.

what: 1. Simulate a lattice of regions and aggregated counts
title: Getting started with SDALGCP2 (simulated example)
code: library(SDALGCP2) library(sf)  set.seed(1) bound <- st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(xmin = 0, ymin = 0, xmax = 20, ymax = 20))) shp   <- st_sf(geometry = st_make_grid(bound, n = c(8, 8)))   # 64 square regions N     <- nrow(shp)  # candidate points inside each region (regular grid) pts      <- sda_points(shp, delta = 1.2, method = 3) phi_grid <- seq(1, 5, length.out = 8) corr     <- precompute_corr(pts, phi_grid)  # true parameters and a latent field drawn at phi = 2.5 Sig   <- 0.5 * corr$R[, , which.min(abs(phi_grid - 2.5))] x1    <- as.numeric(scale(st_coordinates(st_centroid(shp))[, 1])) pop   <- round(runif(N, 500, 3000)) y     <- rpois(N, pop * exp(cbind(1, x1) %*% c(-6, 0.5) +                             as.numeric(t(chol(Sig)) %*% rnorm(N)))) dat   <- data.frame(y = y, x1 = x1, pop = pop)

title: Getting started with SDALGCP2 (simulated example)
text: single call generates points, builds correlation array runs MCML:
code: ctrl <- control_mcmc(n.sim = 6000, burnin = 1500, thin = 6, h = 1.65 / N^(1/6)) fit  <- SDALGCP2(y ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), dat, shp,                  delta = 1.2, phi = phi_grid, method = 3, control.mcmc = ctrl)  summary(fit) confint(fit)

title: Getting started with SDALGCP2 (simulated example)
text: Discrete (region-level) continuous (change--support) prediction. sampler = "laplace" option skips MCMC near-instant approximation.
code: pred_d <- predict(fit, type = "discrete",   sampler = "mcmc",    control.mcmc = ctrl) pred_c <- predict(fit, type = "continuous", sampler = "laplace", cellsize = 1,                   control.mcmc = ctrl)  # exceedance probability of covariate-adjusted relative risk above 1.5 exc <- exceedance(pred_d, thresholds = 1.5)

title: Getting started with SDALGCP2 (simulated example)
text: 64-region example full pipeline (points + correlation + MCML) several times faster SDALGCP::SDALGCPMCML returning estimates. reproducible benchmark lives scripts/compare_vs_SDALGCP.R: Representative output (timings vary): ```
code: # source(system.file("..", "scripts", "compare_vs_SDALGCP.R", package = "SDALGCP2")) True:      beta=(-6, 0.5)      sigma2=0.5    phi=2.5 SDALGCP2:  beta=(-5.738,0.474) sigma2=0.687  phi=1     [1.0s] SDALGCP:   beta=(-5.738,0.474) sigma2=0.687  phi=1     [8.4s] Full-pipeline speedup: ~8x

what: Fast Spatially Discrete Approximation to Log-Gaussian Cox Processes for Aggregated Disease Count Data
title: Fast Spatially Discrete Approximation to Log-Gaussian Cox Processes for Aggregated Disease Count Data
text: faster, modernised successor SDALGCP fitting Spatially Discrete Approximation Log-Gaussian Cox Process (SDA-LGCP) spatially aggregated disease counts (Johnson, Diggle & Giorgi, 2019). SDALGCP2 keeps statistical method moves performance-critical kernels (aggregated correlation assembly, Newton Laplace mode + MALA sampler, Monte Carlo likelihood) C++ via RcppArmadillo + OpenMP — ~8–10× faster end--end 64-region example, returning estimates SDALGCP; drops orphaned/legacy dependencies (geoR, sp, raster, spacetime, mapview, splancs, pdist, maxLik) lean sf/terra/stars stack; ships proper post-fit mapping & diagnostics layer (relative-risk uncertainty maps, exceedance probabilities, φ-profile CI, coefficient plots, residual Moran’s checking); adds -MCMC Laplace fast path prediction. See DESIGN.md full analysis, bottleneck ranking roadmap. Status: active development. spatial fit, prediction visualisation working parity-verified SDALGCP. Planned next: continuous-φ (grid-free) estimation, raster/continuous predictors via intensity-scale aggregation, nugget term, importance-sampling diagnostics, Kronecker-free spatio-temporal path.

title: Fast Spatially Discrete Approximation to Log-Gaussian Cox Processes for Aggregated Disease Count Data
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("olatunjijohnson/SDALGCP2")

title: Fast Spatially Discrete Approximation to Log-Gaussian Cox Processes for Aggregated Disease Count Data
code: library(SDALGCP2) library(sf)  set.seed(1) shp <- st_sf(geometry = st_make_grid(   st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(xmin = 0, ymin = 0, xmax = 20, ymax = 20))), n = c(10, 10))) N <- nrow(shp)  # simulate aggregated counts with a spatial signal + covariate pts  <- sda_points(shp, delta = 1, method = 3) corr <- precompute_corr(pts, seq(1, 6, length.out = 10)) Sig  <- 0.6 * corr$R[, , 5] x1   <- as.numeric(scale(rowSums(st_coordinates(st_centroid(shp))))) pop  <- round(runif(N, 500, 3000)) y    <- rpois(N, pop * exp(cbind(1, x1) %*% c(-6, 0.4) +                            as.numeric(t(chol(Sig)) %*% rnorm(N)))) dat  <- data.frame(y = y, x1 = x1, pop = pop)  # fit (points -> correlation -> Monte Carlo ML), all in one call ctrl <- control_mcmc(n.sim = 6000, burnin = 1500, thin = 6, h = 1.65 / N^(1/6)) fit  <- SDALGCP2(y ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), dat, shp,                  delta = 1, phi = seq(1, 6, length.out = 10), control.mcmc = ctrl) summary(fit)  # predict and map pred_d <- predict(fit, type = "discrete") pred_c <- predict(fit, type = "continuous", sampler = "laplace", cellsize = 0.7)  plot(pred_d, "ARR", midpoint = 1)        # covariate-adjusted relative risk plot(pred_c, "RR", bound = shp)          # continuous relative-risk surface map_exceedance(pred_d, threshold = 1.5)  # hotspot probabilities phi_profile(fit)                         # profile-deviance CI for phi coef_plot(fit)                           # estimates with CIs model_check(fit, pred_d)                 # observed vs fitted + residual Moran's I

what: Estimating the spatial scale: grid vs direct
title: Fast Spatially Discrete Approximation to Log-Gaussian Cox Processes for Aggregated Disease Count Data
text: scale phi enters double integral correlation function pair regions, R_ij(phi) = ∫∫ w_i(x) w_j(y) exp(-||x-y||/phi) dx dy, approximated weighted sum candidate points. SDALGCP2 offers two ways estimate : direct method differentiates integral (closed-form dR/dphi, d²R/dphi²) optimises (beta, sigma², phi) jointly, avoids grid-discretisation error returns proper standard error phi joint Hessian. Full, numerically-verified derivation: math/continuous-phi-derivation.pdf.
code: fit_grid   <- SDALGCP2(..., phi_method = "grid")    # profile over the phi grid (default) fit_direct <- SDALGCP2(..., phi_method = "direct")  # optimise phi continuously

title: Fast Spatially Discrete Approximation to Log-Gaussian Cox Processes for Aggregated Disease Count Data
text: Covariates supplied rasters enter model candidate-point level, inside exponential, aggregated intensity scale log-sum-exp offset statistically correct alternative averaging predictor polygon, biased nonlinear log link whenever covariate varies within regions. SDALGCP2_raster() fits Gauss-Newton fixed point reuses standard machinery. covariate sharp sub-region features (e.g. point exposure sources, ), averaging polygons badly biased intensity-scale aggregation recovers truth:  See scripts/raster_covariates_demo.R DESIGN.md §3b.
code: b_i(beta) = log sum_k w_ik exp( z(x_ik)' beta )      # NOT (mean of z over region)' beta fit <- SDALGCP2_raster(y ~ z + offset(log(pop)), data = dat, my_shp = shp,                        delta = 0.5, rasters = z_raster, phi = phi_grid) True z effect:        1.00 NAIVE areal-average:  1.67   (bias +67%) SDALGCP2_raster:      1.06   (bias  +6%)

what: Fit a spatial SDA-LGCP model — SDALGCP2
title: Fit a spatial SDA-LGCP model — SDALGCP2
text: End--end user entry point: generates candidate points inside region, assembles aggregated region-level correlation array (C++), estimates parameters Monte Carlo maximum likelihood. modern, faster equivalent SDALGCP::SDALGCPMCML() spatial case.

title: Fit a spatial SDA-LGCP model — SDALGCP2
code: SDALGCP2(   formula,   data,   my_shp,   delta,   phi = NULL,   method = 1L,   weighted = FALSE,   pop_shp = NULL,   kappa = 0.5,   par0 = NULL,   control.mcmc = NULL,   phi_method = c("grid", "direct"),   nugget = FALSE,   reanchor = 0L,   rho = 0.55,   giveup = 1000L,   nthreads = 0L,   messages = FALSE )

title: Fit a spatial SDA-LGCP model — SDALGCP2
text: formula model formula, e.g. cases ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)). data data frame model variables (one row per region). my_shp sf polygons (anything coercible via st_as_sf). delta candidate-point spacing. phi numeric vector spatial scale parameters profile; NULL, default grid sqrt(min area) extent/10. method point method: 1 = SSI, 2 = uniform, 3 = regular grid. weighted logical; population-weighted aggregation using pop_shp. pop_shp population-density SpatRaster (needed weighted). kappa Matern smoothness spatial kernel (0.5 default). par0 optional starting values c(beta, sigma2, phi). control.mcmc list control_mcmc. phi_method spatial scale estimated: "grid" (profile supplied phi grid, robust default) "direct" (optimise phi continuously inside MCML objective; exponential kernel ). See package vignette/PDF double-integral derivation. nugget logical; TRUE (requires phi_method = "direct") add unstructured region-level term, fitting covariance \(\sigma^2(R(\phi)+\nu )\) estimating relative nugget \(\nu=\tau^2/\sigma^2\) standard error. Absorbs overdispersion explained spatial structure. reanchor number re-anchoring passes: fitting, latent field re-simulated current optimum model refit, keeps importance weights near-uniform (raises MC effective sample size). 0 (default) fits ; 2-3 usually ample. rho, giveup point-generation controls. nthreads OpenMP threads correlation build. messages logical; print optimiser progress.

title: Fit a spatial SDA-LGCP model — SDALGCP2
text: object class "SDALGCP2".

title: Fit a spatial SDA-LGCP model — SDALGCP2
code: # \donttest{ library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE ## ---- simulate a lattice of regions and aggregated counts ---- set.seed(1) bound <- st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(xmin = 0, ymin = 0, xmax = 20, ymax = 20))) shp   <- st_sf(geometry = st_make_grid(bound, n = c(8, 8))) N     <- nrow(shp)  pts   <- sda_points(shp, delta = 1.2, method = 3)      # regular grid points phi_grid <- seq(1, 5, length.out = 8) corr  <- precompute_corr(pts, phi_grid) Sig   <- 0.5 * corr$R[, , which.min(abs(phi_grid - 2.5))] x1    <- as.numeric(scale(st_coordinates(st_centroid(shp))[, 1])) pop   <- round(runif(N, 500, 3000)) y     <- rpois(N, pop * exp(cbind(1, x1) %*% c(-6, 0.5) +                             as.numeric(t(chol(Sig)) %*% rnorm(N)))) dat   <- data.frame(y = y, x1 = x1, pop = pop)  ## ---- fit ---- ctrl <- control_mcmc(n.sim = 6000, burnin = 1500, thin = 6, h = 1.65 / N^(1/6)) fit  <- SDALGCP2(y ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), dat, shp, delta = 1.2,                  phi = phi_grid, method = 3, control.mcmc = ctrl) summary(fit) #> Call: SDALGCP2(formula = y ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), data = dat, my_shp = shp,  #>     delta = 1.2, phi = phi_grid, method = 3, control.mcmc = ctrl) #>  #> Coefficients: #>             Estimate Std.Err z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept)   -5.966   0.125  -47.62  < 2e-16 *** #> x1             0.824   0.107    7.72  1.2e-14 *** #> sigma^2        0.510   0.207    2.47    0.014 *   #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Spatial scale phi: 1.57143 #> Log-likelihood: 8.33903 #> MC importance-sampling ESS: 1 / 750 (0%);  log-lik MC SE: 0.876 #> Note: sigma^2 is the variance of the latent Gaussian process.  ## ---- predict ---- pred_d <- predict(fit, type = "discrete",   sampler = "mcmc",    control.mcmc = ctrl) pred_c <- predict(fit, type = "continuous", sampler = "laplace", cellsize = 1,                   control.mcmc = ctrl) # }

what: Fit a spatio-temporal SDA-LGCP model (Kronecker-free) — SDALGCP2_ST
title: Fit a spatio-temporal SDA-LGCP model (Kronecker-free) — SDALGCP2_ST
text: Separable space-time SDA-LGCP aggregated counts observed N regions T times. spatial scale phi profiled grid; temporal Matern range nu estimated continuously. likelihood never forms \((NT)\times(NT)\) covariance.

title: Fit a spatio-temporal SDA-LGCP model (Kronecker-free) — SDALGCP2_ST
code: SDALGCP2_ST(   formula,   data,   my_shp,   times,   delta,   phi = NULL,   kappa = 0.5,   kappa_t = 0.5,   method = 3L,   weighted = FALSE,   pop_shp = NULL,   control.mcmc = NULL,   reanchor = 0L,   messages = FALSE )

title: Fit a spatio-temporal SDA-LGCP model (Kronecker-free) — SDALGCP2_ST
text: formula model formula (optional offset(log(pop))). data data frame N*T rows ordered time region (rows (t-1)*N + 1:N time t). my_shp sf polygons N regions. times numeric vector length T observation times. delta candidate-point spacing. phi spatial-scale grid (default geometry). kappa spatial Matern smoothness. kappa_t temporal Matern smoothness. method, weighted, pop_shp point-generation controls. control.mcmc list control_mcmc. reanchor number re-anchoring passes (re-simulate latent field current optimum refit); improves variance-parameter estimates. messages logical; print progress.

title: Fit a spatio-temporal SDA-LGCP model (Kronecker-free) — SDALGCP2_ST
text: object class c("SDALGCP2_ST","SDALGCP2") phi_opt,   nu_opt, coefficient table covariance.

what: Fit an SDA-LGCP with spatially continuous (raster) covariates — SDALGCP2_raster
title: Fit an SDA-LGCP with spatially continuous (raster) covariates — SDALGCP2_raster
text: Covariates supplied rasters enter model candidate-point level aggregated intensity (exp) scale via log-sum-exp offset \(b_i(\beta)=\log\sum_k w_{ik}\exp(z(x_{ik})^\top\beta)\) – statistically correct alternative averaging predictor polygon. Estimation Gauss-Newton fixed point reuses mcml_fit intensity-tilted effective design.

title: Fit an SDA-LGCP with spatially continuous (raster) covariates — SDALGCP2_raster
code: SDALGCP2_raster(   formula,   data,   my_shp,   delta,   rasters,   phi = NULL,   method = 3L,   weighted = FALSE,   pop_shp = NULL,   kappa = 0.5,   tilt_spatial = FALSE,   control.mcmc = NULL,   max_iter = 10L,   tol = 0.001,   messages = FALSE )

title: Fit an SDA-LGCP with spatially continuous (raster) covariates — SDALGCP2_raster
text: formula model formula; right-hand-side names must match raster layer names. response offset(log(pop)) come data. data data frame response offset (one row per region). my_shp sf polygons. delta candidate-point spacing. rasters terra::SpatRaster (object coercible terra::rast) whose layers spatially varying covariates named formula. phi spatial-scale grid (default chosen geometry). method, weighted, pop_shp point-generation controls (see sda_points). kappa Matern smoothness spatial correlation. tilt_spatial logical; FALSE (default) spatial correlation uses population weights precomputed (covariates enter log-sum-exp offset). TRUE, correlation \(R^c(\beta)\) rebuilt iteration intensity-tilted weights \(c_{ik}(\beta)\) log-normal aggregation correction \(\tfrac12\sigma^2(1-R^c_{ii})\) added – fully tilted model (accurate, costly). control.mcmc list control_mcmc. max_iter, tol outer Gauss-Newton iteration controls. messages logical; print progress.

title: Fit an SDA-LGCP with spatially continuous (raster) covariates — SDALGCP2_raster
text: object class "SDALGCP2" (mcml_fit)   extra fields raster = TRUE n_iter.

text: MCML estimate reweights latent samples drawn anchor towards optimum. optimum far anchor weights become uneven estimate unreliable. reports effective sample size importance weights maximiser Monte Carlo standard error maximised log-likelihood, \(\mathrm{SE}\approx\sqrt{1/\mathrm{ESS}-1/B}\).

what: Monte Carlo maximum likelihood estimation for the spatial SDA-LGCP — mcml_fit
title: Monte Carlo maximum likelihood estimation for the spatial SDA-LGCP — mcml_fit
text: Vectorised, Cholesky-based re-implementation SDALGCP::SDALGCPParaEst(). Simulates latent field anchor, profiles importance-sampling MCML objective supplied phi grid.

title: Monte Carlo maximum likelihood estimation for the spatial SDA-LGCP — mcml_fit
code: mcml_fit(   formula,   data,   corr,   par0 = NULL,   control.mcmc = NULL,   phi_method = c("grid", "direct"),   nugget = FALSE,   reanchor = 0L,   reanchor_tol = 0.01,   messages = FALSE )

title: Monte Carlo maximum likelihood estimation for the spatial SDA-LGCP — mcml_fit
text: formula model formula, optionally offset() term. data data frame holding model variables. corr list R (N x N x n_phi correlation array) phi, e.g. precompute_corr. par0 optional starting values c(beta, sigma2, phi); NULL derived Poisson GLM. control.mcmc list control_mcmc (defaults NULL). phi_method "grid" (profile corr phi grid, default) "direct" (optimise phi continuously; exponential/Matern kernel). nugget logical; TRUE (requires phi_method = "direct") add relative nugget, fitting covariance \(\sigma^2(R(\phi)+\nu )\). reanchor number re-anchoring passes (re-simulate latent field current optimum refit) raise importance-sampling ESS. reanchor_tol relative-change tolerance stopping re-anchoring loop. messages logical; print optimiser progress.

title: Monte Carlo maximum likelihood estimation for the spatial SDA-LGCP — mcml_fit
text: object class "SDALGCP2" (estimates, covariance, profile,   latent samples metadata).

text: invisibly, list fitted, residuals moran.

what: Precompute aggregated region-level correlation matrices — precompute_corr
title: Precompute aggregated region-level correlation matrices — precompute_corr
text: Builds \(N \times N \times\) length(phi) array region-level correlations used SDA-LGCP model, $$R(\phi)_{ij} = \sum_{k,l} w_{ik} w_{jl}\, C(\lVert x_{ik}-x_{jl}\rVert; \phi, \kappa)$$ (population-weighted) unweighted mean candidate-point pairs. heavy reduction runs C++ (OpenMP-parallel region pairs); modern, fast replacement SDALGCP::precomputeCorrMatrix().

title: Precompute aggregated region-level correlation matrices — precompute_corr
code: precompute_corr(points, phi, kappa = 0.5, weighted = NULL, nthreads = 0L)

title: Precompute aggregated region-level correlation matrices — precompute_corr
text: points list length \(N\); element holds $xy (\(n_i \times 2\) matrix candidate-point coordinates) , weighted, $weight (length-\(n_i\) vector summing 1). "weighted" "my_shp" attributes produced point-generation step honoured carried . phi numeric vector spatial scale parameters. kappa Matern smoothness; 0.5 (exponential, default), 1.5 2.5 use closed forms C++. weighted logical; NULL (default) taken attr(points, "weighted"). nthreads number OpenMP threads; 0 (default) uses OpenMP runtime default.

title: Precompute aggregated region-level correlation matrices — precompute_corr
text: list R (correlation array) phi, carrying   weighted, my_shp S_coord attributes R.

title: Generate candidate sampling points inside each region — sda_points
text: my_shp sf object POLYGON/MULTIPOLYGON features. delta point spacing (grid step / SSI inhibition distance). method 1 = SSI (default), 2 = uniform random, 3 = regular grid. weighted logical; TRUE, weights population density read pop_shp, otherwise equal weights. pop_shp terra::SpatRaster population density (required weighted = TRUE). rho packing density used choose number points. giveup SSI rejection limit.

title: Generate candidate sampling points inside each region — sda_points
text: list length nrow(my_shp); element xy   weight. Carries "weighted" "my_shp" attributes.

title: SDALGCP2 0.0.0.9000 (development)
text: First development version — faster, modernised successor SDALGCP. Kronecker-free spatio-temporal model (SDALGCP2_ST()): separable space-time SDA-LGCP counts regions several times. likelihood never forms (N*T)x(N*T) covariance, using tr(Rs^-1 M Rt^-1 M') quadratic form N log|Rt| + T log|Rs| log-determinant (verified vs brute-force Kronecker product). Spatial scale grid, temporal Matern range estimated continuously (analytic gradient verified vs numDeriv). geoR. Covariate-tilted raster model (SDALGCP2_raster(tilt_spatial = TRUE)): rebuilds correlation intensity-tilted weights log-normal aggregation correction. General Matern smoothness direct method: corr_and_grad_cpp now provides closed-form phi-derivatives Matern kappa {0.5, 1.5, 2.5}, continuous-phi (phi_method = "direct") estimation works three (exponential ). Derivatives verified finite differences. Nugget / overdispersion term (nugget = TRUE, phi_method = "direct"): fits covariance sigma2 (R(phi) + nu ) estimates relative nugget nu = tau2/sigma2 standard error, absorbing region-level overdispersion beyond spatial structure. Analytic gradient Hessian (including nugget cross terms) verified numerical differentiation. Re-anchored (iterated) MCML (reanchor =): re-simulates latent field current optimum refits, keeping importance weights near-uniform. 64-region example lifts effective sample size ~0% ~96% cuts log-likelihood MC standard error ~100x, correcting variance estimate. Importance-sampling diagnostics (mc_diagnostics(), shown summary()): effective sample size importance weights optimum Monte Carlo SE maximised log-likelihood; warns collapse. Spatially continuous (raster) covariates (SDALGCP2_raster()): covariates given rasters enter LGCP intensity candidate-point level aggregated intensity (exp) scale via log-sum-exp offset b_i(beta) = log sum_k w_ik exp(z(x_ik)'beta) – correct alternative averaging predictor polygons (biased log link). Fit Gauss-Newton fixed point reusing mcml_fit(). sharp-peak covariate, naive areal averaging biased +67% recovers truth ~6%. Continuous-phi (“direct”) estimation (phi_method = "direct"): optimises spatial scale phi continuously inside MCML objective instead profiling grid, using analytic first/second derivatives aggregated double integral (corr_and_grad_cpp). Gives genuine standard error phi joint Hessian avoids grid-boundary artefacts; phi_method = "grid" stays default. Gradient Hessian verified numerical differentiation. full derivation math/continuous-phi-derivation.pdf. Post-fit visualisation & diagnostics: relative-risk / uncertainty / exceedance maps, phi_profile(), coef_plot(), model_check() (residual Moran’s ), report(). C++ (RcppArmadillo + OpenMP) aggregated correlation assembly (precompute_corr). C++ Newton Laplace mode + adaptive MALA sampler (laplace_sampling); bit-identical SDALGCP::Laplace.sampling given mode seed. Vectorised, Cholesky-based Monte Carlo likelihood analytic gradient/Hessian (mcml_fit); matches original ~1e-14 ~10x faster estimation. One-call spatial fit SDALGCP2() (points -> correlation -> MCML); reproduces SDALGCP::SDALGCPMCML estimates ~8x faster end--end 64-region example. terra; drops splancs/sp). Prediction predict.SDALGCP2() (discrete + continuous) MCMC -MCMC Laplace fast path; exceedance() probabilities. Vignette reproducible benchmark scripts using entirely simulated data.
